questions.question: Explain how spooling printer output helps prevent deadlocks.
keyPoints: Spooling makes non-sharable resources appear sharable
Eliminates mutual exclusion condition for printers
Processes send output to spooler rather than directly to printer
Spooler manages exclusive access to printer

questions.question: Compare and contrast deadlock prevention and avoidance approaches, including their advantages and limitations.
keyPoints: Prevention is proactive, avoidance is reactive
Prevention breaks one of the four necessary conditions
Avoidance uses algorithms like Banker's to avoid unsafe states
Prevention can lead to lower resource utilization
Avoidance requires knowledge of future resource needs
